factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with North America water heater volumes declining due to supply chain constraints, while China sales are growing strongly.

evidence.text: In our North America segment, we expect industry residential water heater volumes will be down approximately two percent in 2021 compared with 2020... China sales in 2021 to increase 18 to 20 percent in local currency compared with 2020 due to increased consumer demand.

factors.summary: Order backlog is growing, particularly in North America where customer orders surged in the first quarter of 2021, leading to limited production.

evidence.text: The first quarter of 2021 was particularly challenging for our North America water heater business as severe weather disruptions at our facilities and supply chain constraints limited production. If not for the limited production, based on a surge in customer orders in the first quarter of 2021, we believe our U.S. residential water heater shipments would have increased compared to the same quarter last year.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with three announced price increases in 2021, though this is partly in response to rising material costs.

evidence.text: We announced our third price increase on water heaters in late March, which is effective on June 1, 2021. When fully implemented, the cumulative impact of our three announced price increases is 24 to 27 percent based on the type of water heater.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin products like boilers and water treatment systems, and mid-price range products in China.

evidence.text: We expect to see a low-double-digits increase in our boiler sales in 2021 compared to 2020 due to pandemic-related pent-up demand as well as our new product introductions. We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by 13 to 14 percent in 2021... mix of products sold in China is shifting to more mid-price range products from our historical mix of higher-priced products.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is expected to accelerate, driven by strong demand in North America and China, despite supply chain challenges.

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to increase by between 14 and 15 percent in 2021. Our guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions and assumes the conditions of our business environment and that of our suppliers and customers are similar throughout 2021 to what we have experienced in recent months and does not deteriorate as a result of further restrictions, supply chain bottlenecks or shutdowns.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are mixed, with North America water heater volumes declining but boiler and water treatment product volumes increasing.

evidence.text: We expect industry residential water heater volumes will be down approximately two percent in 2021 compared with 2020... We expect to see a low-double-digits increase in our boiler sales in 2021 compared to 2020... We expect sales of our North America water treatment products to increase by 13 to 14 percent in 2021.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are China, North America's water treatment and boiler segments, while commercial water heaters are declining due to pandemic impacts.

evidence.text: China sales in 2021 to increase 18 to 20 percent in local currency compared with 2020... We expect to see a low-double-digits increase in our boiler sales in 2021... We expect commercial water heater industry volumes will further decline approximately four percent in 2021 as COVID-19 pandemic-impacted businesses continue to delay or defer new construction and discretionary replacement installations.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, particularly steel and logistics costs, with steel prices up 25% since late January 2021.

evidence.text: We continue to experience inflation across our supply chain, particularly steel and logistics costs. The cost of steel has increased 25 percent since late January when we announced our second water heater price which is effective April 1, 2021.

factors.summary: Gross profit margins are slightly lower, but segment margins are expected to improve, particularly in Rest of World.

evidence.text: First quarter gross profit margin of 37.5 percent in 2021 was slightly lower than the gross profit margin of 37.6 percent in the prior-year period... We expect our full-year 2021 Rest of World segment margin to be between seven and eight percent.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

factors.summary: Cost actions, such as reducing SG&A expenses in China, are contributing to higher segment earnings.

evidence.text: Selling, general, and administrative (SG&A) expenses in the first quarter of 2021 were $166.5 million or $7.3 million lower than SG&A expenses of $173.8 million in the first quarter of 2020. The decrease in SG&A expenses in 2021 was primarily due to lower selling expenses and advertising costs in China, resulting from headcount reductions, store closures, cuts in advertising, and other cost-saving measures implemented during 2020.
evidence.subsection: FIRST THREE MONTHS OF 2021 COMPARED TO 2020

factors.summary: Supply chain health is still disrupted, with bottlenecks and delays persisting, particularly in the first quarter of 2021.

evidence.text: Ongoing communications with our suppliers to identify and mitigate potential disruptions and manage inventory levels continue. Bottlenecks in our global supply chain, largely due to the pandemic and severe weather, along with shipping delays, weather-related plant closures, absenteeism and self-quarantine related to the pandemic have further extended our water heater lead times in the first quarter of 2021.

factors.summary: Management has raised forward guidance for 2021, expecting consolidated sales growth of 14-15% and increased EPS.

evidence.text: We expect our consolidated sales to grow between 14 and 15 percent in 2021... We believe we will achieve full-year net earnings of between $2.55 and $2.65 per share.

factors.summary: Management is cautiously optimistic, with a focus on navigating supply chain challenges and executing price increases.

evidence.text: Combining all of these factors, we expect our consolidated sales to increase by between 14 and 15 percent in 2021. Our guidance excludes the potential impacts from future acquisitions and assumes the conditions of our business environment and that of our suppliers and customers are similar throughout 2021 to what we have experienced in recent months and does not deteriorate as a result of further restrictions, supply chain bottlenecks or shutdowns.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include supply chain bottlenecks, potential inflation, and geopolitical factors affecting China.

evidence.text: We have announced three price increases that will go into effect in the first half of 2021, which typically generate pre-buy orders in advance of the price increases. We continue to experience inflation across our supply chain, particularly steel and logistics costs... China currency rates will stay at current levels and which would add approximately $50 million and $3 million to sales and earnings in 2021, respectively.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include steel prices, foreign exchange movements, and supply chain disruptions.

evidence.text: The cost of steel has increased 25 percent since late January when we announced our second water heater price which is effective April 1, 2021... China currency rates will stay at current levels and which would add approximately $50 million and $3 million to sales and earnings in 2021, respectively.

factors.summary: CapEx is expected to remain steady, with projections of $85 to $90 million for 2021.

evidence.text: We project 2021 capital expenditures will be between $85 and $90 million and full year depreciation and amortization expense will be approximately $80 million.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving slightly, with the ratio decreasing from 5.8% to 5.4% as of March 31, 2021.

evidence.text: Our leverage, as measured by the ratio of total debt to total capitalization, calculated excluding operating lease liabilities, was 5.4 percent at March 31, 2021, compared with 5.8 percent at December 31, 2020.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are expected to increase with a $400 million stock repurchase program and a quarterly dividend increase.

evidence.text: Depending on factors such as stock price, working capital requirements and alternative investment opportunities, we expect to spend approximately $400 million on stock repurchases in 2021... Board of Directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.26 per share on our Common Stock and Class A common stock.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is expected to decrease from 2020 levels due to higher investments in working capital.

evidence.text: For the full year 2021, we expect cash provided by operating activities will be between $475 and $500 million, lower than 2020 cash provided by operating activities of $562 million primarily due to higher investments in working capital partially offset by higher earnings compared to the prior year.

factors.summary: FX movements are favorable, particularly in China, contributing approximately $50 million to sales and $3 million to earnings in 2021.

evidence.text: We assume China currency rates will stay at current levels and which would add approximately $50 million and $3 million to sales and earnings in 2021, respectively.
